products.name: XGC2 acados
products.summary: System-level acados solver stack and Python code-generation dependencies for XGC2 projects on Ubuntu 18.04, 20.04, 22.04, and 24.04.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-acados
usage.notes:
CMake consumers should use find_package(xgc2_acados REQUIRED CONFIG).
MATLAB users should run /opt/xgc2/acados/setup_acados.m.
Bionic packages use CasADi 3.5.5 plus vendored dataclasses and typing_extensions backports for Python 3.6 and Ubuntu 18.04 libstdc++ compatibility; newer distributions use CasADi 3.7.2 by default.
setup.bash defaults MPLBACKEND to Agg for headless robot and CI runtime.

products.name: XGC2 Adapter Runtime C++ SDK
products.summary: Generic C++ SDK for capability-first XGC2 AdapterRuntimeLink sessions, generated protocol bindings, fencing, dispatch, and flow control.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install libxgc2-adapter-runtime-client-dev
usage.notes:
CMake consumers use find_package(xgc2_adapter_runtime_client REQUIRED CONFIG)
and link xgc2::adapter_runtime_client. The package exports generated Runtime
Link protobuf C++ headers and xgc2::adapter_runtime_protocol. The -dev
package preserves the existing build interface and pulls the separate
libxgc2-adapter-runtime-client1 SONAME package. Runtime applications depend
only on that ABI package and its generated shlibs dependencies.

products.name: XGC2 Camera Core
products.summary: ROS-independent C++14 V4L2 camera capture library, inspection tool, and development metadata for XGC2 camera adapters.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install libxgc2-camera-dev
usage.notes:
CMake consumers use find_package(xgc2_camera REQUIRED CONFIG) and link
xgc2::camera. This product is intentionally independent of ROS, Catkin,
image decoders, simulators, and device-specific platform integrations.

products.name: XGC2 Math
products.summary: Header-only C++ math library for XGC2 geometry, filters, observers, estimators, and control utilities.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install libxgc2-math-dev
usage.notes:
CMake consumers should use find_package(xgc2_math REQUIRED CONFIG)
and link xgc2_math::math or one of the component targets:
xgc2_math::algebra, xgc2_math::utils, xgc2_math::geometry, xgc2_math::filter,
xgc2_math::observer, xgc2_math::estimation, xgc2_math::optimization,
xgc2_math::trajectory, xgc2_math::control.

products.name: XGC2 MAVLink Router
products.summary: XGC2 fork of mavlink-router packaged as a native Debian executable for Ubuntu 20.04, 22.04, and 24.04.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-mavlink-router

products.name: XGC2 Protocol Schemas
products.summary: Versioned XGC2 capability-first Adapter Runtime Link schemas, semantic payloads, descriptors, and registries for language-neutral protocol development.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-protobuf-dev
usage.notes:
This package installs schema sources and language-neutral discovery metadata only.
Consumers generate or own their language runtime bindings explicitly.

products.name: XGC2 State Machine
products.summary: System-level deterministic C++ state machine development library for XGC2 controllers, planners, and runtime workflows.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install libxgc2-state-machine-dev
usage.notes:
CMake consumers should use find_package(xgc2_state_machine REQUIRED CONFIG)
and link xgc2_state_machine::state_machine. The public include path remains
<state_machine/state_machine.hpp>.

products.name: XGC2 oneTBB
products.summary: System-level oneAPI Threading Building Blocks runtime for XGC2 projects on Ubuntu 20.04, 22.04, and 24.04.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-tbb
usage.notes:
CMake consumers should use find_package(xgc2_tbb REQUIRED CONFIG).
The upstream oneTBB CMake package remains available under /opt/xgc2/tbb.

products.name: XGC2 VRPN Router
products.summary: Protocol-level VRPN tracker relay packaged as a native Debian executable for Ubuntu 20.04, 22.04, and 24.04.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-vrpn-router

products.name: XGC2 AgileX Onboard ROS1
products.summary: ROS Melodic onboard IMU, Scout chassis, communication, and sensor packages for the AgileX onboard computer.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-melodic-xgc2-agilex-onboard-autostart
usage.notes:
The launch defaults match the recovered vehicle startup chain:
port=/dev/imu, baud=115200, topic=/imu/data_raw, frame_id=imu_link.
The IMU deb also installs a one-time udev setup script for /dev/imu:
/opt/ros/melodic/share/agilex_onboard_imu/scripts/install_imu_udev_rule.sh.
The Scout chassis packages are split by ROS package so SDK, messages,
driver, and bringup can be installed independently. The compatible Scout
message definitions are maintained in xgc2-scout-msgs and consumed as the
external Debian package ros-melodic-scout-msgs.
The AgileX swarm bridge package only provides vehicle-specific YAML and
launch files. It consumes the external generic package
ros-melodic-swarm-ros-bridge.
The onboard sensor packages are grouped under onboard/ros1/src/sensors and
split into independent Debian packages for RealSense camera, RealSense
description assets, and RoboSense LiDAR.
The optional autostart package installs systemd units, the IMU udev rule,
lightweight ROS readiness probes, and default swarm bridge configuration.
It enables xgc2-agilex-onboard.target but does not start the target during
installation.
Internal Debian dependencies include >= version constraints so driver and
bringup packages do not silently bind to older SDK/driver packages.
usage.smoke_test:
rospack find agilex_onboard_imu
roslaunch --files agilex_onboard_imu imu_msg.launch
rospack find scout_base
roslaunch --files scout_bringup scout_minimal.launch
rospack find agilex_swarm_ros_bridge
roslaunch --files agilex_swarm_ros_bridge agilex_swarm_ros_bridge.launch

products.name: XGC2 FS150 Robot
products.summary: Real FS150 robot onboard profile, documentation, and runtime dependency aggregation.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-fs150
sudo apt install xgc2-fs150-mavlink-router
usage.notes: The ROS Noetic and XGC2 APT sources must be configured before installing this package.
usage.smoke_test:
test -d /opt/xgc2/robots/fs150/docs
test -f /opt/xgc2/robots/fs150/docs/rk356x_performance_mode.md
dpkg -s xgc2-fs150
dpkg -s xgc2-fs150-mavlink-router
test -f /etc/xgc2/fs150-mavlink-router/router.conf
systemctl is-enabled xgc2-fs150-mavlink-router.service

products.name: XGC2 ROS1 Messages
products.summary: Shared ROS1 message interfaces for XGC2 components.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-ros-msgs
usage.smoke_test:
rospack find state_machine_msgs
rospack find hover_thrust_estimator_msgs
rospack find rigid_state_estimator_msgs

products.name: XGC2 ROS1 Utils
products.summary: ROS1-coupled parameter, namespace, and topic quality helpers for XGC2 runtime packages.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-ros1-utils

products.name: XGC2 ROS1 Robot Adapters
products.summary: Robot-owned PX4 multirotor, Scout Mini, and Mecanum UGV ROS1 semantic adapters.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install \
  ros-noetic-xgc2-px4-multirotor-adapter \
  ros-noetic-xgc2-scout-mini-adapter \
  ros-noetic-xgc2-mecanum-ugv-adapter
usage.notes:
Each robot Adapter is a domain-specific Adapter Runtime application. Core
resolves its provider definition from the installed robot profile catalog;
no robot identity is built into the Adapter base class. This product owns
ROS1-native profile mappings and native safety behavior, while protobuf owns
the cross-language robot and semantic messages.
usage.smoke_test:
rospack find xgc_px4_multirotor_ros1_adapter
rospack find xgc_scout_mini_ros1_adapter
rospack find xgc_mecanum_ugv_ros1_adapter
test -x /opt/ros/noetic/lib/xgc_px4_multirotor_ros1_adapter/xgc_px4_multirotor_ros1_adapter_node
test -x /opt/ros/noetic/lib/xgc_px4_multirotor_ros1_adapter/xgc_px4_multirotor_ros1_adapter_service_helper
test -x /opt/ros/noetic/lib/xgc_scout_mini_ros1_adapter/xgc_scout_mini_ros1_adapter_node
test -x /opt/ros/noetic/lib/xgc_mecanum_ugv_ros1_adapter/xgc_mecanum_ugv_ros1_adapter_node
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/share/xgc_px4_multirotor_ros1_adapter/profiles/ros1/px4-multirotor-ros1-v6.yaml
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/share/xgc_scout_mini_ros1_adapter/profiles/ros1/scout-mini-ros1-v4.yaml
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/share/xgc_mecanum_ugv_ros1_adapter/profiles/ros1/mecanum-ugv-ros1-v1.yaml
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/adapter-definitions/xgc2-px4-multirotor-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/adapter-definitions/xgc2-scout-mini-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/adapter-definitions/xgc2-mecanum-ugv-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/process-definitions/xgc2-px4-multirotor-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/process-definitions/xgc2-scout-mini-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/process-definitions/xgc2-mecanum-ugv-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/robot-adapter-profiles/xgc2-px4-multirotor-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/robot-adapter-profiles/xgc2-scout-mini-ros1-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/robot-adapter-profiles/xgc2-mecanum-ugv-ros1-adapter.json

products.name: XGC2 ROS1 Tools Adapter
products.summary: Generic typed ROS1 topic-publish and service-call capabilities provided as an XGC2 Adapter Runtime application.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-ros1-tools-adapter
usage.notes:
Core or Agent supervises one process per ROS1 native context and supplies a
mode-0600 binary AdapterProcessBootstrap. The process exposes no listener
other than its outbound connection to the target-local Adapter Runtime.
Builds require the exact 0.5.0 SDK and schema inputs. Installed applications
require only the ABI-1 Runtime shared-library package; SDK headers and schema
sources do not participate in the deployment dependency graph.
usage.smoke_test:
rospack find xgc_ros1_tools_adapter
test -x /opt/ros/noetic/lib/xgc_ros1_tools_adapter/xgc_ros1_tools_adapter_node
test -x /opt/ros/noetic/lib/xgc_ros1_tools_adapter/xgc_ros1_tools_adapter_service_helper
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/adapter-definitions/xgc2-ros1-tools-adapter.json
test -f /usr/share/xgc2/process-definitions/xgc2-ros1-tools-adapter.json

products.name: XGC2 Runtime Sync
products.summary: Runtime synchronization coordinator, weak-network telemetry, and swarm sync core for XGC2 ROS1 multi-agent systems.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-runtime-sync

products.name: XGC2 Swarm ROS Bridge
products.summary: ROS Melodic ZeroMQ bridge for selected swarm robot topics.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-melodic-swarm-ros-bridge
usage.notes:
This package is the generic bridge binary. Robot-specific topic and peer
configuration should live in the consuming robot product, not in this
package. The current compiled message set is sensor_msgs/Imu,
geometry_msgs/Twist, and std_msgs/String.
usage.smoke_test:
rospack find swarm_ros_bridge
roslaunch --files swarm_ros_bridge test.launch

products.name: XGC2 Controller Aggregate
products.summary: Aggregate ROS1 controller package that depends on the split XGC2 multirotor and UGV controller products.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-controller
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files px4_multirotor_controller uav_nmpc_controller.launch
roslaunch --files unicycle_ugv_controller ugv_unicycle_nmpc_controller.launch

products.name: XGC2 Multirotor Controller
products.summary: ROS1 PX4 multirotor controller and multirotor reference trajectory packages for XGC2 robots.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-multirotor-controller

products.name: XGC2 UGV Controller
products.summary: ROS1 unicycle UGV controller and planar reference trajectory packages for XGC2 robots.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-ugv-controller
usage.smoke_test:

products.name: XGC2 ROS1 Camera Driver
products.summary: ROS Noetic adapter for the independently released XGC2 Linux camera core.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc-camera-driver
usage.notes:
One process owns one camera and publishes synchronized Image and CameraInfo
messages plus diagnostics. Calibration tools are released independently by
the xgc2-camera-calibration-ros1 product.
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch xgc_camera_driver camera.launch backend:=synthetic pixel_format:=bgr24
rostopic echo -n 1 /usb_cam/camera_info

products.name: XGC2 ROS1 Camera Calibration
products.summary: Independent intrinsic and fixed-world-camera extrinsic calibration tools for ROS Noetic.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-camera-calibration
usage.notes:
Intrinsic calibration is camera-agnostic and can be used for fixed, onboard,
or vehicle-mounted cameras. Fixed-camera extrinsic calibration consumes an
existing CameraInfo or intrinsic YAML as data; it has no package or release
dependency on the intrinsic workflow that may have produced those values.
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files xgc_camera_calibration intrinsic_calibrator.launch
roslaunch --files xgc_camera_calibration extrinsic_calibrator.launch
roslaunch --files xgc_camera_calibration extrinsic_tf.launch

products.name: XGC2 Hover Thrust Estimator
products.summary: Hover thrust estimation package for PX4/MAVROS UAV controllers.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-estimator-hover-thrust

products.name: XGC2 Rigid State Estimators
products.summary: VRPN/IMU rigid state estimation packages for PX4 rotor UAVs and UGVs.

usage.install:

usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files estimator_vrpn_px4_rotor_state vrpn_px4_rotor_state_estimator.launch
roslaunch --files estimator_vrpn_ugv_state vrpn_ugv_state_estimator.launch

products.name: XGC2 Robot Visualization
products.summary: Reusable ROS Noetic RViz marker visualizers for XGC2 robot assets.

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Sim Catalog
products.summary: Non-publishing catalog for the XGC2 Gazebo Classic simulation products.

usage.notes:
This repository records the Gazebo simulation product set and compatible
versions. It does not build, install, or publish an aggregate Debian
package. Consumers install only the independent products they use.

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Classic Scout
products.summary: AgileX Scout Gazebo Classic simulation package for ROS Noetic.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-scout

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Sim Camera
products.summary: Independent fixed-site RGB camera, calibration targets, and RViz AR scene for Gazebo Classic 11.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-camera
usage.notes:
This product owns only an independent fixed-site camera. It does not modify
or depend on any FS150, Scout, UAV, or other vehicle camera model.
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files gazebo_sim_camera static_camera.launch gui:=false
roslaunch --files gazebo_sim_camera intrinsic_calibration_world.launch gui:=false
roslaunch --files gazebo_sim_camera extrinsic_calibration_world.launch gui:=false start_vrpn:=false

products.name: XGC2 CERLAB UAV Simulator
products.summary: CERLAB and Intent-MPC Gazebo Classic UAV simulator and plugins for ROS Noetic.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-cerlab-uav
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files xgc2_cerlab_uav_simulator start.launch
roslaunch --files xgc2_cerlab_uav_simulator px4_start.launch

products.name: XGC2 FS150 SITL
products.summary: FS150 PX4 1.12 iris SITL wrapper with package-owned Gazebo meshes and RViz URDF.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-fs150-sitl
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files gazebo_sim_fs150_sitl fs150.launch gui:=false
roslaunch --files gazebo_sim_fs150_sitl fs150_robot_description.launch

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Sim Mecanum
products.summary: Collision-free ideal planar-velocity Mecanum UGV for Gazebo Classic 11.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-mecanum
usage.notes:
The vehicle is visual-only and has no collision, tire, force, PID, or
ros_control dynamics. Gazebo owns all velocity-to-pose integration.
The product publishes no odometry; the optional VRPN bridge consumes Gazebo
model poses directly.
usage.smoke_test:
roslaunch --files gazebo_sim_mecanum simple.launch gui:=false
rostest gazebo_sim_mecanum ideal_drive.test

products.name: XGC2 PX4 SITL 1.12
products.summary: PX4 v1.12 SITL runtime and Gazebo Classic assets for ROS Noetic.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-px4-1-12
usage.notes: Runtime entry scripts are installed from package scripts; packaging scripts stay under .xgc2/scripts.

products.name: XGC2 PX4 SITL 1.14
products.summary: PX4 v1.14 SITL runtime and Gazebo Classic assets for ROS Noetic.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-px4-1-14
usage.notes: Runtime entry scripts are installed from package scripts; packaging scripts stay under .xgc2/scripts.

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Sim Scenes
products.summary: Reusable Gazebo Classic worlds and the XGC2 scene director.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-worlds ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-scene
usage.notes:
This scene product has two explicit responsibilities. gazebo_sim_worlds
owns reusable world and model assets only. xgc2_gazebo_scene is the scene
director and temporarily retains both the legacy per-model path animator
and the XGC2-managed global SystemPlugin without changing their behavior.
usage.smoke_test:
rospack find gazebo_sim_worlds
rospack find xgc2_gazebo_scene
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/share/gazebo_sim_worlds/worlds/empty/empty.world
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/share/gazebo_sim_worlds/models/corridor/model.sdf
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/lib/libobstaclePathPlugin.so
test -f /opt/ros/noetic/lib/libxgc2_gazebo_scene_system.so

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Sim Visualization
products.summary: Gazebo Classic adapter for reusable XGC2 RViz and Lichtblick robot visualizers.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ros-noetic-xgc2-gazebo-sim-visualization

products.name: XGC2 Gazebo Sim VRPN Bridge
products.summary: Gazebo Classic model pose to VRPN tracker server bridge.

usage.install:

products.name: XGC2 Swarm Sync Sim

usage.install:

sudo apt install ros-noetic-swarm-sync-sim

products.name: XGC2 PX4 SITL 1.16
products.summary: PX4 v1.16 SITL runtime and Gazebo Sim Harmonic assets for ROS Jazzy.

usage.install:

sudo apt install ros-jazzy-xgc2-gz-harmonic-px4-1-16
usage.notes: Runtime entry scripts are installed from package scripts; packaging scripts stay under .xgc2/scripts.

products.name: XGC2 Linux Utils
products.summary: Linux host utility scripts and cpufrequtils performance-governor persistence.

usage.install:

sudo apt install xgc2-utils-linux-performance-mode

products.name: XGC2 Lichtblick Web and Desktop
products.summary: Pinned Lichtblick browser server with an optional Electron desktop package.

usage.install:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install xgc2-lichtblick-web
usage.notes: xgc2-lichtblick-web is the default browser-based package and never opens a desktop window. It recommends the release-matched Foxglove bridge (Noetic on focal, Humble on jammy, and Jazzy on noble) so standard installs add the live-data bridge automatically. The optional xgc2-lichtblick package retains Electron desktop integration and disables its built-in updater; install upgrades through APT.
